strategies.label: Secure remote admin access (guided choices)

fields.description: How would you like to secure remote access?

options.label: Close all public access
options.impact_text: Public SSH/RDP access on ports 22 and 3389 will remain until you manually remove the 0.0.0.0/0 rules. New restricted rules will be added.

options.label: Close public and auto-remove old rules
options.impact_text: All existing 0.0.0.0/0 rules on ports 22 and 3389 will be automatically removed. Make sure you have alternative access (SSM, VPN) before applying.

options.label: Restrict to my IP
options.impact_text: Only traffic from the provided CIDR will be allowed on ports 22 and 3389. All other sources will be denied.

options.label: Restrict to custom CIDR
options.impact_text: Only traffic from the provided CIDR range (for example, office/VPN) will be allowed on ports 22 and 3389.

fields.description: Allowed IPv4 CIDR for SSH/RDP (for example, office or VPN range).
Optional allowed IPv6 CIDR for SSH/RDP.

dependency_checks.message: No specialized dependency checks are available for this strategy yet.

warnings: Restricting or revoking public admin ingress can lock out operators. Confirm SSM, VPN, or bastion access before apply.
strategies.decision_rationale: Family resolver used the compatibility-safe default 'close_public' for strategy 'sg_restrict_public_ports_guided'.

recommendation.rationale: Matrix cell risk_high__criticality_high maps to default recommendation 'exception_review'. Risk is high and business criticality is high. Criticality evidence: data_sensitivity=1.00, privilege_level=0.80, internet_exposure=1.00, matched_signals=none. Relationship context is complete enough for advisory matrix placement.
